title: Debugger State
text: Debugger State The state of the Debugger consists of current settings, watches, and breakpoints. The current state can be saved as a named configuration for repeated use. When you start the Debugger, it attempts to load the configuration file udbg.cnf containing the Debugger preferences and state. If this file does not exist (which is the case the first time you start the Debugger after installing Uniface), it establishes a default state and preferences for the active Debugger session. By default, the Debugger uses the following unnamed states: Default state—created by the Debugger at start-up when the udbg.cnf configuration file is missing. Last-used state—active state at the moment state is saved. By default, the current state is saved when the Debugger is closed. The current state can be saved as a named state to the Debugger’s internal list of states and saved to the configuration file for repeated use. Debugger preferences allow you to load and save Debugger state information and determine the Debugger form windows to be opened at start-up. Related Links Configuring the Uniface Debugger You can configure the Uniface Debugger by setting breakpoints and watches, and selecting preferences and settings from the View menu. The Uniface Debugger settings, watches, and breakpoints make up its state, which can be saved to the configuration file for repeated use. Breakpoints A breakpoint is a marked code line, trigger, error, exception, or condition that forces the application or component to break and the debugger to take control of execution. Watches Watches are designated fields, variables, expressions, and functions that are continuously monitored while the application or component is debugged. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
